description: Prompts for measuring dashmotion structural failure rate. f1-f5 flow + a1-a5 architecture (natural language, ~8-16 nodes; loops, multi-way branches, parallel rails, message bus, nested and sibling boundaries, async edges). m1-m6 mermaid input (v2.2 feature): TB branches+loop, LR nested subgraphs, stateDiagram-v2, edge kinds, adversarial labels, ignored style directives — fidelity measured with check_fidelity.py against the embedded source.

prompts.prompt: Use dashmotion to visualize our CI/CD pipeline: a commit triggers lint, unit tests and integration tests in parallel; all three merge into building the Docker image; then a security scan; then a manual approval gate (approved / rejected — rejected notifies the author and ends); approved deploys a canary; canary metrics are checked — if they regress we roll back and return to the approval gate, if they pass we promote to production and send a Slack notification.

Use dashmotion to visualize this order workflow: user submits an order; validate payment and reserve inventory in parallel; both merge into a fraud-review wait timer; after the timer a decision — flagged orders go to manual review which can loop back to the timer once, clean orders proceed; ship the order; then send a confirmation email and update analytics in parallel; end.

Use dashmotion to draw our signup state machine: visitor fills the form; validation errors loop back to the form; on success create the account and send a verification email; wait up to 24h (timer); if the link is clicked the account becomes Active and onboarding starts; if not clicked, one resend is allowed (loop back to send email); after the resend window expires the account is purged (separate end state).

Use dashmotion to visualize our nightly ETL: the scheduler kicks off extraction from three sources in parallel — the billing API, a Postgres dump, and S3 event files; the three extracts merge into normalize; a data-quality gate decides — failing batches go to quarantine and alert the data team (end), passing batches continue; transform into the star schema; load the warehouse; then refresh BI dashboards and notify downstream consumers in parallel; end.

Use dashmotion to draw our incident response runbook: an alert fires; triage decides severity three ways — P1 pages the on-call and opens a war room, P2 creates a ticket for the next business day, P3 is logged only and ends; P1 and P2 both lead to mitigation; verify the fix — if verification fails loop back to mitigation; once verified, write the postmortem and close the incident.

Use dashmotion to draw our SaaS architecture on AWS and animate the main request path: a React frontend served behind CloudFront; API Gateway routes to an Auth Service (Go), an Order Service (Node.js) and a Notification Service (Python) inside a private subnet; PostgreSQL on RDS and Redis on ElastiCache as the data layer; Cognito handles auth; static assets live in S3. Animate an order request from the client all the way to PostgreSQL, plus the auth flow to Cognito.

Use dashmotion to draw our Kubernetes microservices platform: an NGINX ingress in front; users, catalog, cart and payments services inside the 'shop' namespace; a Kafka bus between the services and two async workers (email worker, analytics worker); PostgreSQL for orders and MongoDB for the catalog; Prometheus and Grafana in an observability namespace. Animate a checkout request from ingress through cart and payments to PostgreSQL, and an async event from payments through Kafka to the email worker.

Use dashmotion to draw our multi-region failover setup: Route 53 with health checks at the top; region us-east-1 contains an ALB and two app servers and the RDS primary; region eu-west-1 contains an ALB and two app servers and an RDS read replica; cross-region database replication between the two RDS instances; CloudWatch monitors both regions. Animate a normal user request into us-east-1 down to the primary, and the replication flow from primary to replica.

Use dashmotion to draw our serverless event-driven backend: clients hit API Gateway which invokes an Orders Lambda writing to DynamoDB; DynamoDB streams feed EventBridge; EventBridge fans out to an SQS queue consumed by a Fulfillment Lambda that calls SES to email the customer, and to a Firehose delivery stream into S3 for analytics; static frontend on S3 + CloudFront. Animate the synchronous order request and the asynchronous fulfillment event as separate journeys.

Use dashmotion to draw our hybrid on-prem + cloud architecture: the on-premises datacenter contains Active Directory, a legacy ERP system and a file server; a site-to-site VPN connects it to AWS; inside AWS a private subnet holds DMS replicating the ERP database to RDS, plus two app servers behind an internal ALB; IAM Identity Center federates with the on-prem AD. Animate the ERP data sync journey from on-prem through the VPN and DMS into RDS, and a user auth flow from AD to IAM Identity Center.

Use dashmotion to animate this mermaid diagram:

```mermaid
flowchart TB
    A[Receive ticket] --> B{Severity?}
    B -->|P1| C[Page on-call]
    B -->|P2| D[Create incident]
    B -->|P3| E[Queue for triage]
    C --> F[Mitigate]
    D --> F
    E --> G[Weekly review]
    F --> H{Resolved?}
    H -->|yes| I[Postmortem]
    H -->|no| F
    I --> J[Close ticket]
    G --> J
```

Use dashmotion to animate this mermaid diagram:

```mermaid
flowchart LR
    U[User] --> CDN
    subgraph EDGE["Edge"]
        CDN[CloudFront CDN]
    end
    subgraph VPC["AWS VPC"]
        subgraph PUB["Public subnet"]
            ALB[Application Load Balancer]
        end
        subgraph PRIV["Private subnet"]
            API[API service]
            WORK[Worker]
        end
        DB[(PostgreSQL)]
        Q[SQS queue]
    end
    CDN --> ALB --> API
    API --> DB
    API -.-> Q
    Q -.-> WORK
    WORK --> DB
```

Use dashmotion to animate this mermaid diagram:

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Draft
    Draft --> Review : submit
    Review --> Draft : changes requested
    Review --> Approved : approve
    Approved --> Published : publish
    Published --> Archived : expire
    Archived --> [*]
    Review --> Review : add comment
```

Use dashmotion to animate this mermaid diagram:

```mermaid
flowchart TB
    P[Producer] ==> V[Validator]
    V --> T[Transformer]
    T -.-> Q[Queue]
    Q -.-> C[Consumer]
    C --> S[(Store)]
```

Use dashmotion to animate this mermaid diagram:

```mermaid
flowchart TB
    A["Validate input -- strict mode"] --> B{"是否合规?"}
    B -->|"yes & clean"| C["Write to 'main' branch"]
    B -->|no| D["Reject — notify <admin>"]
    D --> A
```

Use dashmotion to animate this mermaid diagram:

```mermaid
flowchart TB
    classDef hot fill:#ff0000,stroke:#00ff00
    A[Gateway]:::hot --> B[Service]
    style B fill:#ffff00
    linkStyle 0 stroke:#0000ff
```
